result:
Design decisions emitted for SvelteKit:

- **Hook**: `afterNavigate` — only hook satisfying afterCancellation + afterRedirects that also fires on initial mount (onNavigate excludes 'enter')
- **Wrapping**: renderless `<DatadogRumRouter />` component mounted in root `+layout.svelte` (no createRouter factory in SvelteKit)
- **View name**: `route-id` family — `navigation.to.route.id` delivers the parameterized pattern verbatim, fallback to pathname when null
- **Package**: new `rum-sveltekit`
- **Reference**: `rum-vue` (plugin shape, event-based subscription, pure view-name helper all transfer)
- **SSR**: afterNavigate is inherently client-only; belt-and-braces `browser` check around startView

selectedHook.name: afterNavigate
selectedHook.rationale: Candidates with afterCancellation:true — onNavigate, afterNavigate, page ($app/state via $effect), navigating ($app/state), page ($app/stores legacy). Rule 1 (afterCancellation:true required) eliminates beforeNavigate. Rule 2 (prefer afterRedirects:true) eliminates 'navigating'. Rule 3 (prefer afterFetch:false AND afterRender:false) is unsatisfiable — all SvelteKit post-cancellation hooks fire after load/fetch, because SvelteKit runs load() before committing the navigation by design; higher-priority rules (already met) win. Tie-breakers remaining: onNavigate, afterNavigate, page reactive, page legacy. By strict 'earliest in lifecycle' onNavigate would win, BUT onNavigate's navigation.type explicitly excludes 'enter' — it does NOT fire on initial page load, so using it would drop the first RUM view. afterNavigate fires uniformly on initial mount (type 'enter') AND every subsequent client navigation, gives the final post-redirect NavigationTarget with navigation.to.route.id, and is still an event-based callback (not reactive state diffing). The page-state hooks require $effect plumbing and comparing previous/next route ids to detect a navigation — more complex with no timing benefit over afterNavigate. Legacy $app/stores is deprecated since SvelteKit 2.12. afterNavigate wins on coverage + simplicity; its 'afterRender:true' timing is the unavoidable SvelteKit client-side cost.

wrappingStrategy.target: A Svelte component (e.g. `<DatadogRumRouter />`) that calls afterNavigate during component initialization; users place it once in their root `src/routes/+layout.svelte`. The component renders nothing.
wrappingStrategy.rationale: afterNavigate MUST be invoked during Svelte component initialization — it has no standalone/imperative API. SvelteKit has no user-controlled router factory (routing is file-based and framework-owned), so there is no createRouter to wrap like in Vue/React. There is no DI container to inject into like Angular. The idiomatic Svelte way to attach app-wide lifecycle behaviour is a component that lives in the root layout; this is exactly how View Transitions and analytics libraries are wired into SvelteKit. A renderless .svelte component keeps the public API ergonomic ('<DatadogRumRouter />') and matches SvelteKit user expectations while guaranteeing the afterNavigate subscription runs in component-init context.

viewNameAlgorithm.rationale: afterNavigate provides navigation.to.route.id — a string that is the parameterized route pattern mirroring the filesystem (e.g. '/blog/[slug]', '/[org]/[repo]/tree/[branch]/[...file]', '/(app)/dashboard'). This is the ideal route-id case: the framework hands us exactly the collapsed pattern we want as the RUM view name, no matched-record iteration and no param-substitution reconstruction needed. Post-processing is minimal: (a) fall back to navigation.to.url.pathname when route.id is null (unmatched/404), (b) keep bracket syntax, matchers, optional/rest segments, and route groups verbatim — Stage 1 examples explicitly preserve '(app)' groups and '[p=matcher]' qualifiers in the view name, matching SvelteKit's own route.id shape.

referenceImplementation.rationale: Closest match across all three axes: (1) Plugin shape — a top-level factory plugin with a `router: boolean` option that sets `trackViewsManually = true` at onInit, plus onRumInit/onRumStart deferred-subscriber plumbing in a companion module; rum-vue's vuePlugin.ts is nearly a line-for-line template. (2) Subscription pattern — both subscribe to a single event-based hook that delivers post-redirect, post-cancellation route info (Vue's router.afterEach ↔ SvelteKit's afterNavigate), and both must ignore query-only changes and failed navigations. (3) View-name algorithm — same shape of computeViewName: given framework-provided route data, produce a parameterized string; rum-vue's startVueRouterView.ts splits the concern nicely between the subscription site and the pure-function name computation. rum-react is less relevant (wraps a user-called createBrowserRouter factory), rum-nextjs is less relevant (App Router uses params+pathname reconstruction). The wrapping surface differs — rum-vue wraps vue-router's createRouter, rum-sveltekit ships a renderless Svelte component — but everything on the RUM-facing side (plugin + onRumInit queue + startView call + view-name helper + spec layout) transfers directly.

ssr.handling: afterNavigate is a client-only navigation hook: SvelteKit guarantees it never fires during server rendering. The renderless component can be imported on the server safely because the afterNavigate callback simply won't execute there. Defense-in-depth: gate any direct RUM public-API calls (e.g. the startView invocation in startSvelteKitView) behind `import { browser } from '$app/environment'` check before calling rumPublicApi.startView, so that even if a user mis-mounts the component in a server-only path the integration no-ops instead of throwing on missing window globals. datadogRum.init() itself is the caller's responsibility to gate with `if (browser)` — document this in the integration README, consistent with how every SvelteKit analytics SDK guides users.

structured_output.notes: Key gotchas Stage 3 must handle: (1) afterNavigate fires on INITIAL page load with navigation.type === 'enter' and navigation.from === null — treat this as the initial view, do not skip it. (2) Skip query-only changes by comparing navigation.from?.url.pathname to navigation.to.url.pathname (consistent with rum-vue's `from.path === to.path` guard and the SDK's default automatic view tracking that ignores query strings). (3) navigation.to.route.id can be null (no route matched, 404) — fall back to navigation.to.url.pathname. (4) Hash-router mode (kit.router.type: 'hash', added 2.14): afterNavigate still fires with route.id correct, but pathname lives in url.hash — the route-id path means this is transparent to us since we trust route.id. (5) View-Transitions users rely on onNavigate returning a Promise/cleanup; we intentionally do NOT subscribe to onNavigate, so no interference. (6) Plugin must set initConfiguration.trackViewsManually = true only when configuration.router === true (mirror rum-vue). (7) getConfigurationTelemetry must return { router: !!configuration.router }. (8) Peer deps: svelte ^5.0.0 (for the .svelte component with modern syntax) and @sveltejs/kit ^2.12.0 (covers afterNavigate's stable navigation shape and the $app/state era); mark both as optional peer deps like rum-vue does for vue/vue-router. (9) Entry points: main.ts exporting the plugin + config types, plus a separate component entry (e.g. 'sveltekit-router' subpath) exporting the DatadogRumRouter.svelte component — mirroring rum-vue's dual `main.ts` + `vueRouter.ts` entries. (10) Package name choice: 'rum-sveltekit' over 'rum-svelte' because plain Svelte has no router — this package is meaningful only with SvelteKit routing.
